sales.title:
NumisCorner - Auction 6, Lot 3 — Attica, Tetradrachm, ca. 485-480 BC, Athens, Silver, NGC, Ch VF 4/5-5/5
Head of Athena in Archaïc style, wearing Attic crested helmet and round earings., Owl standing right, head facing; behind, olive
sales.description:
Attica, Tetradrachm, ca. 485-480 BC, Athens, Silver, NGC, Ch VF 4/5-5/5
Head of Athena in Archaïc style, wearing Attic crested helmet and round earings., Owl standing right, head facing; behind, olive sprig with berry; before, legend AΘE; all within incuse square., Graded NGC Ch VF Strike: 4/5 Surface: 5/5. Attractive example of an archaic Athenian tetradrachm. Seltman Group Gi.
DENOMINATION:Tetradrachm                    
YEAR:ca. 485-480 BC                    
COIN CONDITION:Ch VF 4/5-5/5  ...

sales.title:
Heritage World Coin Auctions - NYINC Signature Sale 3130, Lot 36022 — Ancients
ATTICA. Athens. Ca. 510/500-480 BC. AR light-weight specimen tetradrachm (21mm, 16.38 gm, 11h). NGC VF 4/5 - 5/5, flan flaw. Head of Athena right, wearing crested Attic helmet, the crest box
sales.description:
Ancients
ATTICA. Athens. Ca. 510/500-480 BC. AR light-weight specimen tetradrachm (21mm, 16.38 gm, 11h). NGC VF 4/5 - 5/5, flan flaw. Head of Athena right, wearing crested Attic helmet, the crest box ornamented with chevron pattern / AΘΕ, owl standing right with closed wings, head facing; drooping olive sprig with two leaves and one berry behind, all within incuse square. Seltman Group C. HGC 4, 1590 (ca. 500/490-485/480 BC). A charming archaic rendition of the iconic owl.

Ex James E. Beach, ...
